speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Where is the King?

speaker: BEDFORD
text: The King himself is rode to view their battle.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Of fighting men they have full threescore thousand.

speaker: EXETER
text: There’s five to one. Besides, they all are fresh.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: God’s arm strike with us! ’Tis a fearful odds.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: God be wi’ you, princes all. I’ll to my charge.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: If we no more meet till we meet in heaven,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Then joyfully, my noble Lord of Bedford,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My dear Lord Gloucester, and my good Lord Exeter,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And my kind kinsman, warriors all, adieu.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Farewell, good Salisbury, and good luck go with thee.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: And yet I do thee wrong to mind thee of it,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: For thou art framed of the firm truth of valor.

speaker: EXETER
text: Farewell, kind lord. Fight valiantly today.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: He is as full of valor as of kindness,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Princely in both.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: O, that we now had here

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: But one ten thousand of those men in England

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That do no work today.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What’s he that wishes so?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My cousin Westmoreland? No, my fair cousin.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If we are marked to die, we are enough

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To do our country loss; and if to live,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The fewer men, the greater share of honor.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: God’s will, I pray thee wish not one man more.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: By Jove, I am not covetous for gold,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor care I who doth feed upon my cost;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: It yearns me not if men my garments wear;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Such outward things dwell not in my desires.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But if it be a sin to covet honor,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I am the most offending soul alive.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No, ’faith, my coz, wish not a man from England.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: God’s peace, I would not lose so great an honor

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As one man more, methinks, would share from me,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For the best hope I have. O, do not wish one more!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Rather proclaim it, Westmoreland, through my host,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That he which hath no stomach to this fight,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let him depart. His passport shall be made,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And crowns for convoy put into his purse.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We would not die in that man’s company

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That fears his fellowship to die with us.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This day is called the feast of Crispian.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He that outlives this day and comes safe home

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Will stand o’ tiptoe when this day is named

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And rouse him at the name of Crispian.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He that shall see this day, and live old age,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Will yearly on the vigil feast his neighbors

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And say “Tomorrow is Saint Crispian.”

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then will he strip his sleeve and show his scars.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Old men forget; yet all shall be forgot,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But he’ll remember with advantages

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What feats he did that day. Then shall our names,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Familiar in his mouth as household words,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Harry the King, Bedford and Exeter,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Warwick and Talbot, Salisbury and Gloucester,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be in their flowing cups freshly remembered.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This story shall the good man teach his son,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And Crispin Crispian shall ne’er go by,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From this day to the ending of the world,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But we in it shall be rememberèd—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We few, we happy few, we band of brothers;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For he today that sheds his blood with me

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall be my brother; be he ne’er so vile,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This day shall gentle his condition;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And gentlemen in England now abed

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall think themselves accursed they were not here,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And hold their manhoods cheap whiles any speaks

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That fought with us upon Saint Crispin’s day.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My sovereign lord, bestow yourself with speed.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The French are bravely in their battles set,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And will with all expedience charge on us.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: All things are ready if our minds be so.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Perish the man whose mind is backward now!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou dost not wish more help from England, coz?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: God’s will, my liege, would you and I alone,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Without more help, could fight this royal battle!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, now thou hast unwished five thousand men,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Which likes me better than to wish us one.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: You know your places. God be with you all.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Once more I come to know of thee, King Harry,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: If for thy ransom thou wilt now compound,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Before thy most assurèd overthrow.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: For certainly thou art so near the gulf

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Thou needs must be englutted. Besides, in mercy,

speaker: MONTJOY
text: The Constable desires thee thou wilt mind

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Thy followers of repentance, that their souls

speaker: MONTJOY
text: May make a peaceful and a sweet retire

speaker: MONTJOY
text: From off these fields where, wretches, their poor bodies

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Must lie and fester.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Who hath sent thee now?

speaker: MONTJOY
text: The Constable of France.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I pray thee bear my former answer back.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Bid them achieve me and then sell my bones.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good God, why should they mock poorfellows thus?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The man that once did sell the lion’s skin

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: While the beast lived was killed with hunting him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A many of our bodies shall no doubt

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Find native graves, upon the which, I trust,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall witness live in brass of this day’s work.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And those that leave their valiant bones in France,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Dying like men, though buried in your dunghills,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They shall be famed; for there the sun shall greet them

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And draw their honors reeking up to heaven,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Leaving their earthly parts to choke your clime,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The smell whereof shall breed a plague in France.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Mark, then, abounding valor in our English,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That being dead, like to the bullet’s crazing,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Break out into a second course of mischief,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Killing in relapse of mortality.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let me speak proudly: tell the Constable

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We are but warriors for the working day;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Our gayness and our gilt are all besmirched

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With rainy marching in the painful field.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: There’s not a piece of feather in our host—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good argument, I hope, we will not fly—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And time hath worn us into slovenry.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But, by the Mass, our hearts are in the trim,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And my poor soldiers tell me, yet ere night

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They’ll be in fresher robes, or they will pluck

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The gay new coats o’er the French soldiers’ heads

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And turn them out of service. If they do this,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As, if God please, they shall, my ransom then

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Will soon be levied. Herald, save thou thy labor.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come thou no more for ransom, gentle herald.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They shall have none, I swear, but these my joints,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Which, if they have, as I will leave ’em them,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall yield them little, tell the Constable.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: I shall, King Harry. And so fare thee well.

speaker: MONTJOY
text: Thou never shalt hear herald anymore.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I fear thou wilt once more come again

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: for a ransom.

speaker: YORK
text: My lord, most humbly on my knee I beg

speaker: YORK
text: The leading of the vaward.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Take it, brave York.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now, soldiers, march away,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And how Thou pleasest, God, dispose the day.
